# Terms of Service — Solar Operator

*Last updated: June 4, 2026*

These Terms of Service ("Terms") are a binding agreement between you ("you,"
"the Operator") and Solar Operator ("we," "us," "the Service"), a Vermont
limited liability company. They govern your access to and use of the Solar
Operator software, website, browser extension, and related services available
at **solaroperator.org**. By creating an account, completing checkout, or
otherwise using the Service, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do
not use the Service.

## 1. What the Service does

Solar Operator is a software-as-a-service product that automates quarterly
net-metering credit reporting for community solar operators in Vermont. The
Service pulls billing and production data from your Green Mountain Power ("GMP")
utility accounts — by way of the Solar Operator Sync browser extension and your
own authenticated GMP session — and uses it to generate NEPOOL-format Excel
reports. We then deliver those reports by email to the recipients you designate
for each of your clients. The Service is currently built for GMP data only.

We may add, change, or remove features over time. We will make reasonable
efforts to maintain availability of the Service, but we do not guarantee that it
will be uninterrupted, error-free, or available at any particular time.

## 2. Eligibility and accounts

You must be at least 18 years old and authorized to enter into these Terms on
behalf of yourself or the entity you represent. You are responsible for
maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and magic-link login
emails, and for all activity that occurs under your account. Notify us promptly
at **admin@solaroperator.org** if you believe your account has been accessed
without authorization.

## 3. Fees and billing

Use of the Service requires payment of the following fees, billed through our
payment processor (Stripe):

- **Setup fee:** a one-time charge of **$250** at the time you complete onboarding.
- **Subscription fee:** **$45 per array per month**, billed monthly in advance,
  based on the number of solar arrays active in your account.

Your array count may change as you add or remove arrays. We will adjust your
monthly subscription quantity accordingly, and changes take effect on your next
billing cycle. All fees are stated in U.S. dollars and are exclusive of any
taxes, which are your responsibility where applicable.

## 4. Cancellation and refunds

You may cancel your subscription at any time, effective at the end of the
then-current monthly billing period. After cancellation you will retain access
through the end of the period you have already paid for, and you will not be
charged for subsequent periods. **Subscription fees already paid are not
refundable, and the one-time setup fee is non-refundable**, except where required
by law. To cancel, use the controls in your dashboard or email
**admin@solaroperator.org**.

## 5. Your data and ownership

As between you and us, **all data you provide or that we collect on your behalf —
including your client records, array configurations, GMP billing and production
data, and the reports we generate — remains your property** (the "Operator
Data"). We claim no ownership of the Operator Data.

You grant us a limited, non-exclusive license to access, process, store, and
transmit the Operator Data solely as needed to provide and improve the Service,
to generate and deliver your reports, and as otherwise described in our Privacy
Policy. We will not sell your data or share it with third parties except as
necessary to operate the Service (for example, our hosting, payment, and email
providers) or as required by law.

You may export your reports at any time and may request deletion of your data as
described in the Privacy Policy. Our handling of personal and utility data is
governed by the Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by
reference.

## 6. Your responsibilities

You are responsible for the accuracy and lawful use of the information you give
us and the actions you take through the Service, including:

- **NEPOOL-GIS identifiers.** You are solely responsible for providing and
  verifying the correct NEPOOL-GIS ID for each array. **We do not independently
  validate NEPOOL-GIS IDs and make no warranty that any ID you supply, or that
  appears in a generated report, is correct.** An incorrect ID may cause reports
  to be inaccurate or misattributed, and that is your responsibility to catch and
  correct.
- **Authorization to access utility data.** You represent that you have the right
  and any necessary consent to access the GMP accounts and data you connect to
  the Service, including consent from your clients where their accounts are
  involved.
- **Report review.** The Service automates report preparation, but you are
  responsible for reviewing each report for accuracy before relying on it or
  forwarding it to clients, regulators, or the utility.
- **Compliance.** You will use the Service in compliance with all applicable
  laws, utility tariffs, and program rules, and you will not misuse the Service,
  attempt to disrupt it, or access it in any unauthorized way.

## 7. Third-party services

The Service depends on third parties, including Green Mountain Power, Stripe, and
our email and hosting providers. We are not responsible for the availability,
accuracy, or actions of those third parties. Changes by GMP to its portal, data,
or APIs may affect the Service, and we may need to adapt or temporarily suspend
features in response.

## 8. Disclaimer of warranties

**The Service is provided "as is" and "as available," without warranties of any
kind, whether express, implied, or statutory.** To the fullest extent permitted
by law, we disclaim all implied warranties, including merchantability, fitness
for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. We do not warrant that the
Service will meet your requirements, that reports or data will be accurate,
complete, or error-free, or that any defects will be corrected. Without limiting
the foregoing, **we make no warranty as to the correctness of any NEPOOL-GIS ID
or of the net-metering credit figures derived from utility data**, which depend
on inputs and third-party sources outside our control.

## 9. Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, in no event will we be liable for any
indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for any
loss of profits, revenue, data, or goodwill, arising out of or relating to the
Service or these Terms, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such
damages. Our total aggregate liability for any claim arising out of or relating
to the Service or these Terms will not exceed the total fees you paid to us in
the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

## 10. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify and hold us harmless from any claims, losses, liabilities,
and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of your use of
the Service, your Operator Data, your breach of these Terms, or your violation of
any law or the rights of a third party.

## 11. Suspension and termination

We may suspend or terminate your access to the Service if you breach these Terms,
fail to pay fees when due, or use the Service in a way that risks harm to us, our
other customers, or third parties. You may stop using the Service at any time by
cancelling as described above.

## 12. Changes to these Terms

We may update these Terms from time to time. If we make material changes, we will
notify registered customers by email and post the updated version at
solaroperator.org. Your continued use of the Service after changes take effect
constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

## 13. Governing law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Vermont, without regard to
its conflict-of-laws rules. You agree that the state and federal courts located
in Vermont will have exclusive jurisdiction over any dispute arising out of or
relating to these Terms or the Service.

## 14. Contact

Questions about these Terms? Email **admin@solaroperator.org**.
